message: Fix release artifact extraction and naming

 {"id":"bd-1tv.8","title":"Document the artifact naming reconciliation system","description":"# Purpose

Create comprehensive documentation explaining:
1. The artifact naming problem and solution
2. How to configure repos.d for install.sh compatibility
3. How dsr generates dual-name assets
4. Troubleshooting naming issues

## Background

This system is non-obvious. Future maintainers (including AI agents) need clear documentation to understand why dual-naming exists and how to configure it.

## Documentation Structure

### 1. docs/ARTIFACT_NAMING.md (new file)

\`\`\`markdown
# Artifact Naming in dsr

## Overview

dsr produces release artifacts with TWO naming conventions:
1. **Versioned names** for explicit version downloads
2. **Install.sh-compatible names** for curl|bash installers

This ensures compatibility with both use cases.

## The Problem

Install scripts expect specific asset names:
\`\`\`bash
# install.sh expects:
TAR="myapp-${TARGET}.${EXT}"  # e.g., myapp-darwin-arm64.tar.gz
\`\`\`

But versioned naming is useful for explicit downloads:
\`\`\`
myapp-v1.2.3-darwin-arm64.tar.gz
\`\`\`

dsr solves this by uploading BOTH.

## Configuration

### Basic (auto-detection)
\`\`\`yaml
# repos.d/myapp.yaml
artifact_naming: "\${name}-\${version}-\${os}-\${arch}"
install_script_path: install.sh  # dsr parses this
\`\`\`

### Explicit (override)
\`\`\`yaml
artifact_naming: "\${name}-\${version}-\${os}-\${arch}"
install_script_compat: "\${name}-\${os}-\${arch}"
\`\`\`

## Validation

Check naming consistency:
\`\`\`bash
dsr repos validate
dsr repos validate --repo myapp
\`\`\`

## Troubleshooting

### Install.sh falls back to building from source
**Cause:** Asset name doesn't match install.sh expectation
**Fix:** Add `install_script_compat` to repos.d config

### "No prebuilt binary for TARGET"
**Cause:** Compat name not uploaded
**Fix:** Ensure dsr release was run (not just dsr build)

...
\`\`\`

### 2. Update docs/CLI_CONTRACT.md

Add section on artifact naming:
- New config fields
- validate subcommand
- doctor artifact_naming check

### 3. Update README.md

Add brief mention:
> dsr automatically handles artifact naming for install.sh compatibility.
> See docs/ARTIFACT_NAMING.md for details.

### 4. In-code comments

Add comments explaining the dual-naming logic:
\`\`\`bash
# In release_upload_with_compat:
# We upload each artifact twice:
# 1. Versioned name (e.g., tool-v1.2.3-linux-amd64.tar.gz)
#    - For users who want a specific version
# 2. Compat name (e.g., tool-linux-amd64.tar.gz)
#    - For install.sh scripts that don't include version in URL
# This mirrors what GH Actions produces with the "latest" redirect.
\`\`\`

## Content to Cover

### Why dual naming?
- GH Actions implicitly handles this via "latest" release
- dsr needs to be explicit
- Both versioned and unversioned downloads are valid use cases

### How it works
- artifact_naming_generate_dual() creates both names
- release_upload_with_compat() uploads both
- Checksums include both names
- Signatures cover both names

### Configuration options
- install_script_compat: explicit override
- install_script_path: auto-detection
- Fallback: strip version from artifact_naming

### Validation
- dsr repos validate: check before release
- dsr doctor: includes naming check
- Error messages guide to solution

### Edge cases
- Same names: only upload once
- Windows .zip vs .tar.gz
- Custom install scripts
- Projects without install.sh

## Files to Create/Modify

- `docs/ARTIFACT_NAMING.md` — New comprehensive guide
- `docs/CLI_CONTRACT.md` — Add config and command docs
- `README.md` — Brief mention and link
- `src/artifact_naming.sh` — In-code comments
- `src/act_runner.sh` — In-code comments

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] docs/ARTIFACT_NAMING.md created with full explanation
- [ ] CLI_CONTRACT.md updated with new config fields
- [ ] README mentions artifact naming with link
- [ ] Key functions have explaining comments
- [ ] Documentation covers all edge cases
- [ ] Troubleshooting section addresses common issues","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T00:06:15.139625564Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T00:06:56.331321225Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1tv.8","depends_on_id":"bd-1tv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T00:06:15.139625564Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-1tv.8","depends_on_id":"bd-1tv.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T00:06:56.331268806Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-1tv.9","title":"Ensure remote build hosts have cloned repos with valid git state","description":"# Purpose

During the release session, we encountered failures because:
1. Repos weren't cloned on remote hosts (mmini, wlap)
2. Repos existed but had broken git state (missing .git directory)
3. Working trees had uncommitted changes blocking git pull

dsr needs to handle all these cases automatically.

## Background

When building via SSH to mmini (macOS) and wlap (Windows), dsr assumes:
1. The repo is cloned at the path specified in `host_paths`
2. Git is functional
3. The repo is at the correct version

These assumptions often fail:
- First-time builds: repo never cloned
- Synced via rsync: .git directory stripped
- Other agents: uncommitted changes

## Implementation

### Add pre-build host validation

\`\`\`bash
ensure_remote_repo_ready() {
    local host="$1"
    local repo_path="$2"
    local repo_url="$3"
    local version="$4"
    
    log_info "Ensuring $repo_path on $host is ready..."
    
    # Check if path exists
    if ! ssh "$host" "test -d '$repo_path'" 2>/dev/null; then
        log_info "Cloning repo on $host..."
        ssh "$host" "git clone '$repo_url' '$repo_path'"
    fi
    
    # Check if .git exists
    if ! ssh "$host" "test -d '$repo_path/.git'" 2>/dev/null; then
        log_warn "Missing .git on $host, re-cloning..."
        ssh "$host" "rm -rf '$repo_path' && git clone '$repo_url' '$repo_path'"
    fi
    
    # Ensure we can pull (handle dirty working tree)
    if ! ssh "$host" "cd '$repo_path' && git pull" 2>/dev/null; then
        log_warn "Pull failed on $host, stashing changes..."
        ssh "$host" "cd '$repo_path' && git stash && git pull"
    fi
    
    # Checkout the target version
    ssh "$host" "cd '$repo_path' && git checkout '$version'"
}
\`\`\`

### Integrate with build flow

Before any remote build:
\`\`\`bash
# In build command before SSH to host
ensure_remote_repo_ready "$host" "$remote_path" "$repo_url" "$version"
\`\`\`

### Handle Windows path differences

Windows uses backslashes and different path format:
\`\`\`bash
# Convert path for Windows hosts
if [[ "$host" == "wlap" ]]; then
    # C:/Users/jeffr/projects/foo → C:\Users\jeffr\projects\foo
    remote_path="${remote_path//\//\\}"
fi
\`\`\`

## Error Recovery

If clone/pull fails:
1. Log clear error message
2. Suggest manual intervention
3. Don't proceed with build (fail fast)

## Testing

Add test that:
1. Removes repo from mock host
2. Runs build
3. Verifies repo was auto-cloned
4. Verifies build succeeded

## Files to Modify

- `src/act_runner.sh` — Add `ensure_remote_repo_ready` function
- Build command flow — Call function before SSH builds
- `src/host_health.sh` — Optionally add repo-ready check

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] First-time build auto-clones repo
- [ ] Broken git state auto-repaired
- [ ] Dirty working tree handled (stash)
- [ ] Windows path handling correct
- [ ] Clear error messages on failure","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T00:09:50.447851513Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T01:03:09.253072760Z","closed_at":"2026-02-02T01:03:09.252981017Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1tv.9","depends_on_id":"bd-20r","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T00:32:08.946390466Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-kg5","title":"Add native build unit tests for SSH command construction","description":"## Purpose
Create comprehensive unit tests for the native build SSH command construction in `src/act_runner.sh`. These tests ensure command generation is correct before fixing the bugs.

## Test File
`scripts/tests/test_act_runner_native.sh`

## Mocking Strategy
The existing test infrastructure uses function override mocking. Apply the same pattern:

```bash
# Mock SSH to capture commands without executing
_original_act_ssh_exec="$(_act_ssh_exec 2>/dev/null || echo '')"
_act_ssh_exec() {
    local host="$1" cmd="$2"
    # Record the command for verification
    echo "$cmd" >> "$TEST_SSH_COMMANDS_FILE"
    # Return mock success/failure based on test case
    return ${MOCK_SSH_EXIT_CODE:-0}
}

# Mock SCP similarly
scp() {
    echo "SCP: $*" >> "$TEST_SCP_COMMANDS_FILE"
    return ${MOCK_SCP_EXIT_CODE:-0}
}
```

## Test Cases

### 1. Command Construction Tests
```bash
test_unix_command_construction() {
    # Given: Unix host (mmini), path, build command
    # When: _act_build_remote_cmd called
    # Then: Command uses single quotes, && chaining, export syntax
    # Verify: cd '/path' && export VAR=val && cargo build
}

test_windows_command_construction() {
    # Given: Windows host (wlap), path, build command
    # When: _act_build_remote_cmd called  
    # Then: Command uses PowerShell syntax
    # Verify: pwsh -Command "Set-Location 'path'; $env:VAR='val'; cargo build"
}

test_path_with_spaces() {
    # Given: Path like '/Users/John Doe/projects'
    # Verify: Properly escaped in both Unix and Windows formats
}

test_path_with_special_chars() {
    # Given: Paths with quotes, ampersands, dollar signs
    # Verify: Properly escaped for shell interpretation
}

test_env_var_export_unix() {
    # Given: CARGO_TERM_COLOR=always RUST_BACKTRACE=1
    # Verify: export CARGO_TERM_COLOR=always; export RUST_BACKTRACE=1;
}

test_env_var_export_windows() {
    # Given: Same env vars
    # Verify: $env:CARGO_TERM_COLOR='always'; $env:RUST_BACKTRACE='1';
}
```

### 2. SCP Command Tests
```bash
test_scp_quoting_unix() {
    # Given: Remote path /Users/jemanuel/projects/tool/target/release/binary
    # When: SCP command constructed
    # Then: No nested quotes that confuse scp
    # Verify: scp mmini:/Users/.../binary ./local_path
}

test_scp_quoting_windows() {
    # Given: Remote path C:/Users/jeffr/projects/tool/target/release/binary.exe
    # When: SCP command constructed
    # Then: Windows path handled correctly
}

test_scp_with_spaces_in_path() {
    # Given: /Users/John Doe/My Projects/tool/binary
    # Verify: Path properly quoted for scp
}

test_artifact_path_construction() {
    # Test language-specific binary paths:
    # Rust: target/release/<binary>
    # Go: <binary> or bin/<binary>
    # Add .exe for Windows
}
```

### 3. Host Detection Tests
```bash
test_host_platform_detection() {
    # Verify act_get_native_host returns:
    # linux/* -> trj
    # darwin/* -> mmini
    # windows/* -> wlap
}

test_windows_host_detection() {
    # Verify _is_windows_host returns true for wlap
    # Verify command construction switches to PowerShell
}
```

### 4. Error Handling Tests
```bash
test_ssh_exit_code_propagation() {
    # Given: SSH returns exit code 1
    # Then: act_run_native_build returns 1
    # And: Status is 'failed' in result JSON
}

test_ssh_timeout_handling() {
    # Given: SSH times out (exit 124)
    # Then: Status is 'timeout'
    # And: Appropriate error message logged
}

test_scp_failure_handling() {
    # Given: Build succeeds but SCP fails
    # Then: Status is 'failed' with exit code 7
    # And: artifact_path is empty in result
}
```

## Test Infrastructure
Use existing patterns from `scripts/tests/test_act_runner.sh`:
- `setup_test_env()` for isolated test environment
- `teardown_test_env()` for cleanup
- `assert_equals`, `assert_contains` helpers
- Temporary directories for mock files

## Logging Requirements
- Log the exact command being tested
- Log expected vs actual output on failure
- Use `_log_debug` for verbose test output when DSR_TEST_VERBOSE=1

## Acceptance Criteria
- All tests pass with `set -uo pipefail`
- Tests run in < 5 seconds (no real SSH/SCP calls)
- ShellCheck passes on test file at warning level
- Tests document expected behavior with Given/When/Then
- 100% coverage of command construction code paths","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T23:14:42.875165454Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:16:04.535351680Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T03:16:04.535332013Z","close_reason":"Added comprehensive unit tests for native build SSH command construction (30 tests covering Unix/Windows command construction, path handling, SCP commands, host detection, error handling, and JSON result structure)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-n16","title":"Add source code sync to remote build hosts before native builds","description":"## Problem
Native builds on remote hosts (mmini, wlap) fail if source code doesn't exist at the expected path. Currently, users must manually rsync/scp source code before running `dsr build`.

## Discovery Context
During testing `dsr build remote_compilation_helper`:
- macOS build failed: `zsh:cd:1: no such file or directory: /data/projects/remote_compilation_helper`
- Windows build failed: `The system cannot find the path specified.`
- Required manual: `rsync -avz --exclude='.git' --exclude='target' /data/projects/repo/ mmini:~/projects/repo/`

## Current Behavior
`act_run_native_build` assumes:
1. Source exists at `local_path` (from config) on remote host
2. OR `host_paths.<host>` specifies alternate location
3. User has pre-synced the code manually

## Proposed Solution
Add automatic source sync before native builds:

### Option A: Built-in rsync (Recommended)
```bash
# In act_run_native_build, before build command:
_act_sync_source() {
    local host="$1" local_path="$2" remote_path="$3"
    local exclude_patterns=(
        '--exclude=.git'
        '--exclude=target'
        '--exclude=node_modules'
        '--exclude=.beads'
        '--exclude=*.log'
    )
    
    rsync -az --delete "${exclude_patterns[@]}" \
        "$local_path/" "$host":"$remote_path/"
}
```

### Option B: Configurable sync command
Allow repos.d/<tool>.yaml to specify:
```yaml
sync:
  enabled: true
  method: rsync  # or scp, git-push
  exclude:
    - .git
    - target
    - node_modules
```

## Implementation Details

### For macOS (mmini)
- rsync works natively
- Use `~/projects/<tool>` as default remote path
- Handle symlinks correctly (`-L` flag if needed)

### For Windows (wlap)
- rsync may not be available (check first)
- Fallback to tar + scp + untar:
  ```bash
  tar czf - --exclude=.git . | ssh wlap 'cd /c/Users/jeffr/projects/tool && tar xzf -'
  ```
- Or use WSL's rsync if available

### Performance Considerations
- Use `--delete` to remove stale files
- Use `-z` for compression over slow links
- Cache exclude patterns from .gitignore
- Add `--dry-run` support for preview

## Acceptance Criteria
1. `dsr build <tool>` auto-syncs source before native builds
2. Sync skipped for local builds (act on trj)
3. `--no-sync` flag to disable auto-sync
4. `--sync-only` flag to sync without building
5. Sync respects .gitignore patterns
6. Handles paths with spaces correctly
7. Works on both macOS and Windows remotes
8. Progress indication for large syncs
9. Sync errors fail fast with clear message

## Test Requirements

### Unit Tests
- Test exclude pattern construction from .gitignore
- Test sync command generation for different hosts
- Test Windows fallback detection

### E2E Tests
- Test sync + build cycle on mmini
- Test sync + build cycle on wlap
- Test `--no-sync` bypasses sync
- Test `--sync-only` syncs without building
- Verify synced files match source

### Logging
- Log sync start/end with duration
- Log bytes transferred
- Log any excluded files (verbose mode)

## Files to Modify
- `src/act_runner.sh`: add _act_sync_source function
- `dsr`: add --no-sync, --sync-only flags to cmd_build
- `docs/CLI_CONTRACT.md`: document new flags","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-01T00:49:26.399107836Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:20:57.254331463Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T03:20:57.254312788Z","close_reason":"Implemented source code sync to remote build hosts. Added act_sync_sources() with rsync and tar fallback, --no-sync and --sync-only flags to dsr build, auto-sync before native builds with .gitignore support","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-ov2","title":"Test infrastructure: coverage reporting and metrics","description":"# Test Infrastructure: Coverage Reporting and Metrics

## Purpose
Provide function-level and line-level coverage estimation for Bash scripts without external tools.

## Approach
Since Bash doesn't have built-in coverage tools like gcov, we use:
1. **Function enumeration**: Parse exported functions from each module
2. **Test mapping**: Track which tests call which functions
3. **Execution tracing**: Optional PS4-based tracing for line coverage

## Coverage Metrics

### 1. Function Coverage
- List all exported functions per module
- Track function calls during test execution
- Report: `functions_tested / functions_total`

### 2. Test-to-Function Mapping
```json
{
  "config.sh": {
    "config_init": ["test_config.sh::test_init"],
    "config_load": ["test_config.sh::test_load", "test_e2e.sh::test_full_flow"],
    "config_validate": []  // Not tested\!
  }
}
```

### 3. Gap Analysis
- Functions with zero test coverage
- Functions with only happy-path coverage
- Modules with low overall coverage

## Implementation

### Coverage Script
```bash
./scripts/coverage.sh [--module config.sh] [--format json|text]
```

### Output
```
=== Coverage Report ===
Module            Functions  Tested  Coverage
config.sh              8       6     75%
github.sh             10       7     70%
build_state.sh        15      15    100%
...
TOTAL                 50      42     84%

Untested functions:
- config.sh: config_validate
- github.sh: gh_create_release, gh_upload_asset, gh_delete_release
```

## CI Integration
- Run after test suite
- Fail if coverage drops below threshold
- Generate JSON for trending

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Parse exported functions from all src/*.sh
- [ ] Track function calls during test runs
- [ ] Generate human-readable report
- [ ] Generate JSON for CI
- [ ] Flag untested functions
- [ ] Optional threshold enforcement","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:35:27.277939425Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-31T01:01:48.483631392Z","closed_at":"2026-01-31T01:01:48.483612977Z","close_reason":"Implemented scripts/coverage.sh: function-level coverage reporting with text/JSON output, threshold enforcement, and verbose mode","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ov2","depends_on_id":"bd-130","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:35:27.277939425Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-sfn","title":"E2E: dsr health (real behavior)","description":"# E2E: dsr health (real behavior)

## Purpose
Verify host health checks run against real local host data and return schema-valid output.

## Approach
- Use temp hosts.yaml with one local host definition.
- Run `dsr health` and `dsr health check <host>`.

## Logging
- Record hosts config used.
- On failure, dump stdout/stderr and cache files.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Local host reports healthy (or actionable warnings).
- [ ] --json output schema-valid.

 {"id":"bd-v78","title":"Unit tests for toolchain_detect.sh module","description":"# Unit Tests for toolchain_detect.sh Module

## Module Overview
toolchain_detect.sh provides safe toolchain detection for installers:
- `toolchain_detect` - Detect specific toolchain (rust/go/bun/node)
- `toolchain_detect_all` - Detect all supported toolchains
- `toolchain_ensure` - Verify toolchain available
- `toolchain_install` - Install if missing (with consent)
- `_tc_version_ge` - Compare semver versions

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Detection Tests
- Detect Rust via `rustc --version`
- Detect Go via `go version`
- Detect Bun via `bun --version`
- Detect Node via `node --version`
- Handle missing toolchain gracefully

### 2. Version Comparison Tests
- 1.70.0 >= 1.70.0 → true
- 1.71.0 >= 1.70.0 → true
- 1.69.0 >= 1.70.0 → false
- Handle pre-release versions (1.70.0-beta)
- Handle missing version string

### 3. Platform Detection Tests
- Detect linux/amd64 correctly
- Detect darwin/arm64 correctly
- Detect windows/amd64 correctly
- Handle unknown platform

### 4. Installation Tests (with mocks)
- Prompt for consent in interactive mode
- Skip prompt in non-interactive mode
- Never overwrite existing installation
- Log installation commands (dry-run)

### 5. Ensure Tests
- Pass when toolchain present and version OK
- Fail when toolchain missing
- Fail when version too old
- Suggest installation command

## Test Approach
- Mock toolchain commands where needed
- Test version comparison logic directly
- Use NON_INTERACTIVE=true for consent bypass
- Verify no actual installations occur","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:34:04.144853096Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T19:00:05.378100130Z","closed_at":"2026-01-30T19:00:05.377592634Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-v78","depends_on_id":"bd-130","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T18:34:04.144853096Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

+    _act_sha256_zip_entry() {
+        local zip_file="$1"
+        local entry="$2"
+
+        if command -v sha256sum &>/dev/null; then
+            unzip -p "$zip_file" "$entry" 2>/dev/null | sha256sum | awk '{print $1}'
+            return 0
+        fi
+
+        if command -v shasum &>/dev/null; then
+            unzip -p "$zip_file" "$entry" 2>/dev/null | shasum -a 256 | awk '{print $1}'
+            return 0
+        fi
+
+        return 3
+    }
+
+    _act_zip_entry_size() {
+        local zip_file="$1"
+        local entry="$2"
+        unzip -p "$zip_file" "$entry" 2>/dev/null | wc -c | tr -d ' '
+    }
+
+    _act_manifest_add_zip_entries() {
+        local zip_file="$1"
+        local target="$2"
+        local version_tag="$manifest_version"
+        local version_stripped="${manifest_version#v}"
+
+        if ! command -v unzip &>/dev/null; then
+            _log_warn "unzip not available; treating $zip_file as artifact"
+            _act_manifest_add_file "$zip_file" "$target"
+            return 0
+        fi
+
+        local entries
+        entries=$(unzip -Z1 "$zip_file" 2>/dev/null)
+        if [[ -z "$entries" ]]; then
+            _log_warn "No entries found in zip artifact: $zip_file"
+            return 0
+        fi
+
+        local -a matched_entries=()
+        while IFS= read -r entry; do
+            [[ -z "$entry" || "$entry" == */ ]] && continue
+            if [[ "$entry" == *"$version_tag"* || "$entry" == *"$version_stripped"* ]]; then
+                matched_entries+=("$entry")
+            fi
+        done <<< "$entries"
+
+        local -a use_entries=()
+        if [[ ${#matched_entries[@]} -gt 0 ]]; then
+            use_entries=("${matched_entries[@]}")
+        else
+            while IFS= read -r entry; do
+                [[ -z "$entry" || "$entry" == */ ]] && continue
+                use_entries+=("$entry")
+            done <<< "$entries"
+        fi
+
+        local entry
+        for entry in "${use_entries[@]}"; do
+            local name
+            name=$(basename "$entry")
+
+            case "$name" in
+                *.minisig|*.sig|*.sha256|*.sha512|SHA256SUMS*|*.sbom.*|*.intoto.jsonl)
+                    continue
+                    ;;
+            esac
+
+            local seen_key="${zip_file}::${entry}"
+            for seen in "${seen_paths[@]}"; do
+                [[ "$seen" == "$seen_key" ]] && continue 2
+            done
+
+            local sha size format
+            sha=$(_act_sha256_zip_entry "$zip_file" "$entry" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
+            size=$(_act_zip_entry_size "$zip_file" "$entry")
+            format=$(_act_archive_format "$name")
+
+            if [[ -z "$sha" ]]; then
+                _log_warn "Unable to compute SHA256 for artifact: $zip_file::$entry"
+                continue
+            fi
+            if [[ -z "$size" || "$size" -le 0 ]]; then
+                _log_warn "Unable to determine size for artifact: $zip_file::$entry"
+                continue
+            fi
+
+            local artifact_json
+            artifact_json=$(jq -nc \
+                --arg name "$name" \
+                --arg target "$target" \
+                --arg sha "$sha" \
+                --argjson size "$size" \
+                --arg format "$format" \
+                '{
+                    name: $name,
+                    target: $target,
+                    sha256: $sha,
+                    size_bytes: $size,
+                    archive_format: $format,
+                    signed: false,
+                    signature_file: ""
+                }')
+
+            artifacts+=("$artifact_json")
+            seen_paths+=("$seen_key")
+        done
+    }
